opg.title: Ádám's Conjecture
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture Every digraph with at least one directed cycle has an arc whose reversal reduces the number of directed cycles.

opg.title: Antidirected trees in digraphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: An antidirected tree is an orientation of a tree in which every vertex has either indegree 0 or outdergree 0. Conjecture Let $ D $ be a digraph. If $ |A(D)| > (k-2) |V(D)| $ , then $ D $ contains every antidirected tree of order $ k $ .

opg.title: Arc-disjoint directed cycles in regular directed graphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture If $ G $ is a $ k $ -regular directed graph with no parallel arcs, then $ G $ contains a collection of $ {k+1 \choose 2} $ arc-disjoint directed cycles.

opg.title: Arc-disjoint out-branching and in-branching
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture There exists an integer $ k $ such that every $ k $ -arc-strong digraph $ D $ with specified vertices $ u $ and $ v $ contains an out-branching rooted at $ u $ and an in-branching rooted at $ v $ which are arc-disjoint.

opg.title: Arc-disjoint strongly connected spanning subdigraphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory
opg.statement: Conjecture There exists an ineteger $ k $ so that every $ k $ -arc-connected digraph contains a pair of arc-disjoint strongly connected spanning subdigraphs?

opg.title: Caccetta-Häggkvist Conjecture
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture Every simple digraph of order $ n $ with minimum outdegree at least $ r $ has a cycle with length at most $ \lceil n/r\rceil $

opg.title: Circular flow number of regular class 1 graphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Coloring > Nowhere-zero flows
opg.statement: A nowhere-zero $ r $ -flow $ (D(G),\phi) $ on $ G $ is an orientation $ D $ of $ G $ together with a function $ \phi $ from the edge set of $ G $ into the real numbers such that $ 1 \leq |\phi(e)| \leq r-1 $ , for all $ e \in E(G) $ , and $ \sum_{e \in E^+(v)}\phi(e) = \sum_{e \in E^-(v)}\phi(e), \textrm{ for all } v \in V(G) $ . The circular flow number of $ G $ is inf $ \{ r | G $ has a nowhere-zero $ r $ -flow $ \} $ , and it is denoted by $ F_c(G) $ . A graph with maximum vertex degree $ k $ is a class 1 graph if its edge chromatic number is $ k $ . Conjecture Let $ t \geq 1 $ be an intege

opg.title: Circular flow numbers of $r$-graphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory
opg.statement: A nowhere-zero $ r $ -flow $ (D(G),\phi) $ on $ G $ is an orientation $ D $ of $ G $ together with a function $ \phi $ from the edge set of $ G $ into the real numbers such that $ 1 \leq |\phi(e)| \leq r-1 $ , for all $ e \in E(G) $ , and $ \sum_{e \in E^+(v)}\phi(e) = \sum_{e \in E^-(v)}\phi(e), \textrm{ for all } v \in V(G) $ . A $ (2t+1) $ -regular graph $ G $ is a $ (2t+1) $ -graph if $ |\partial_G(X)| \geq 2t+1 $ for every $ X \subseteq V(G) $ with $ |X| $ odd. Conjecture Let $ t > 1 $ be an integer. If $ G $ is a $ (2t+1) $ -graph, then $ F_c(G) \leq 2 + \frac{2}{t} $ .

opg.title: Cyclic spanning subdigraph with small cyclomatic number
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture Let $ D $ be a digraph all of whose strong components are nontrivial. Then $ D $ contains a cyclic spanning subdigraph with cyclomatic number at most $ \alpha(D) $ .

opg.title: Decomposing an even tournament in directed paths.
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs > Tournaments
opg.statement: Conjecture Every tournament $ D $ on an even number of vertices can be decomposed into $ \sum_{v\in V}\max\{0,d^+(v)-d^-(v)\} $ directed paths.

opg.title: Decomposing k-arc-strong tournament into k spanning strong digraphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs > Tournaments
opg.statement: Conjecture Every k-arc-strong tournament decomposes into k spanning strong digraphs.

opg.title: Directed path of length twice the minimum outdegree
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture Every oriented graph with minimum outdegree $ k $ contains a directed path of length $ 2k $ .

opg.title: Edge-disjoint Hamilton cycles in highly strongly connected tournaments.
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs > Tournaments
opg.statement: Conjecture For every $ k\geq 2 $ , there is an integer $ f(k) $ so that every strongly $ f(k) $ -connected tournament has $ k $ edge-disjoint Hamilton cycles.

opg.title: Erdős-Posa property for long directed cycles
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture Let $ \ell \geq 2 $ be an integer. For every integer $ n\geq 0 $ , there exists an integer $ t_n=t_n(\ell) $ such that for every digraph $ D $ , either $ D $ has a $ n $ pairwise-disjoint directed cycles of length at least $ \ell $ , or there exists a set $ T $ of at most $ t_n $ vertices such that $ D-T $ has no directed cycles of length at least $ \ell $ .

opg.title: Hamilton cycle in small d-diregular graphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: An directed graph is $ k $ -diregular if every vertex has indegree and outdegree at least $ k $ . Conjecture For $ d >2 $ , every $ d $ -diregular oriented graph on at most $ 4d+1 $ vertices has a Hamilton cycle.

opg.title: Highly arc transitive two ended digraphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Infinite Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture If $ G $ is a highly arc transitive digraph with two ends, then every tile of $ G $ is a disjoint union of complete bipartite graphs.

opg.title: Hoàng-Reed Conjecture
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture Every digraph in which each vertex has outdegree at least $ k $ contains $ k $ directed cycles $ C_1, \ldots, C_k $ such that $ C_j $ meets $ \cup_{i=1}^{j-1}C_i $ in at most one vertex, $ 2 \leq j \leq k $ .

opg.title: Jaeger's modular orientation conjecture
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Coloring > Nowhere-zero flows
opg.statement: Conjecture Every $ 4k $ - edge-connected graph can be oriented so that $ {\mathit indegree}(v) - {\mathit outdegree}(v) \cong 0 $ (mod $ 2k+1 $ ) for every vertex $ v $ .

opg.title: Large acyclic induced subdigraph in a planar oriented graph.
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture Every planar oriented graph $ D $ has an acyclic induced subdigraph of order at least $ \frac{3}{5} |V(D)| $ .

opg.title: Linial-Berge path partition duality
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Coloring
opg.statement: Conjecture The minimum $ k $ -norm of a path partition on a directed graph $ D $ is no more than the maximal size of an induced $ k $ -colorable subgraph.

opg.title: Long directed cycles in diregular digraphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture Every strong oriented graph in which each vertex has indegree and outdegree at least $ d $ contains a directed cycle of length at least $ 2d+1 $ .

opg.title: Minimum number of arc-disjoint transitive subtournaments of order 3 in a tournament
opg.subject: Graph Theory
opg.statement: Conjecture If $ T $ is a tournament of order $ n $ , then it contains $ \left \lceil n(n-1)/6 - n/3\right\rceil $ arc-disjoint transitive subtournaments of order 3.

opg.title: Monochromatic reachability or rainbow triangles
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs > Tournaments
opg.statement: In an edge-colored digraph, we say that a subgraph is rainbow if all its edges have distinct colors, and monochromatic if all its edges have the same color. Problem Let $ G $ be a tournament with edges colored from a set of three colors. Is it true that $ G $ must have either a rainbow directed cycle of length three or a vertex $ v $ so that every other vertex can be reached from $ v $ by a monochromatic (directed) path?

opg.title: Monochromatic reachability in arc-colored digraphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture For every $ k $ , there exists an integer $ f(k) $ such that if $ D $ is a digraph whose arcs are colored with $ k $ colors, then $ D $ has a $ S $ set which is the union of $ f(k) $ stables sets so that every vertex has a monochromatic path to some vertex in $ S $ .

opg.title: Non-edges vs. feedback edge sets in digraphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: For any simple digraph $ G $ , we let $ \gamma(G) $ be the number of unordered pairs of nonadjacent vertices (i.e. the number of non-edges), and $ \beta(G) $ be the size of the smallest feedback edge set . Conjecture If $ G $ is a simple digraph without directed cycles of length $ \le 3 $ , then $ \beta(G) \le \frac{1}{2} \gamma(G) $ .

opg.title: Oriented chromatic number of planar graphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Coloring > Vertex coloring
opg.statement: An oriented colouring of an oriented graph is assignment $ c $ of colours to the vertices such that no two arcs receive ordered pairs of colours $ (c_1,c_2) $ and $ (c_2,c_1) $ . It is equivalent to a homomorphism of the digraph onto some tournament of order $ k $ . Problem What is the maximal possible oriented chromatic number of an oriented planar graph?

opg.title: Oriented trees in n-chromatic digraphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture Every digraph with chromatic number at least $ 2k-2 $ contains every oriented tree of order $ k $ as a subdigraph.

opg.title: The Two Color Conjecture
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture If $ G $ is an orientation of a simple planar graph, then there is a partition of $ V(G) $ into $ \{X_1,X_2\} $ so that the graph induced by $ X_i $ is acyclic for $ i=1,2 $ .

opg.title: Partitionning a tournament into k-strongly connected subtournaments.
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs > Tournaments
opg.statement: Problem Let $ k_1, \dots , k_p $ be positve integer Does there exists an integer $ g(k_1, \dots , k_p) $ such that every $ g(k_1, \dots , k_p) $ -strong tournament $ T $ admits a partition $ (V_1\dots , V_p) $ of its vertex set such that the subtournament induced by $ V_i $ is a non-trivial $ k_i $ -strong for all $ 1\leq i\leq p $ .

opg.title: PTAS for feedback arc set in tournaments
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Graph Algorithms
opg.statement: Question Is there a polynomial time approximation scheme for the feedback arc set problem for the class of tournaments?

opg.title: Seymour's Second Neighbourhood Conjecture
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture Any oriented graph has a vertex whose outdegree is at most its second outdegree.

opg.title: Splitting a digraph with minimum outdegree constraints
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Problem Is there a minimum integer $ f(d) $ such that the vertices of any digraph with minimum outdegree $ d $ can be partitioned into two classes so that the minimum outdegree of the subgraph induced by each class is at least $ d $ ?

opg.title: Stable set meeting all longest directed paths.
opg.subject: Graph Theory
opg.statement: Conjecture Every digraph has a stable set meeting all longest directed paths

opg.title: Subdivision of a transitive tournament in digraphs with large outdegree.
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture For all $ k $ there is an integer  $ f(k) $ such that every digraph of minimum outdegree at least  $ f(k) $ contains a subdivision of a transitive tournament of order $ k $ .

opg.title: The Bermond-Thomassen Conjecture
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture For every positive integer $ k $ , every digraph with minimum out-degree at least $ 2k-1 $ contains $ k $ disjoint cycles.

opg.title: Universal highly arc transitive digraphs
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Infinite Graphs
opg.statement: An alternating walk in a digraph is a walk $ v_0,e_1,v_1,\ldots,v_m $ so that the vertex $ v_i $ is either the head of both $ e_i $ and $ e_{i+1} $ or the tail of both $ e_i $ and $ e_{i+1} $ for every $ 1 \le i \le m-1 $ . A digraph is universal if for every pair of edges $ e,f $ , there is an alternating walk containing both $ e $ and $ f $ Question Does there exist a locally finite highly arc transitive digraph which is universal?

opg.title: Woodall's Conjecture
opg.subject: Graph Theory > Directed Graphs
opg.statement: Conjecture If $ G $ is a directed graph with smallest directed cut of size $ k $ , then $ G $ has $ k $ disjoint dijoins.

arxiv.title: Problem 2
arxiv.statement: Determine $\lim_{r\rightarrow\infty}\frac{c_{r}}{r}$.
arxiv.paper_title: Openly disjoint cycles and directed tree-width of regular digraphs

arxiv.title: Informal Question on optimal directed tree-width constant for regular digraphs
arxiv.statement: What is the best-possible constant $\varepsilon>0$ such that every $r$-regular digraph $D$ has directed tree-width at least $\varepsilon r$?
arxiv.paper_title: Openly disjoint cycles and directed tree-width of regular digraphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4.1
arxiv.statement: There exists an $\varepsilon>0$ and infinitely many values of $n$ for which there exists a connected vertex transitive digraph of order $n$, whose perimeter gap is at least $\varepsilon n$.
arxiv.paper_title: Long cycles in vertex transitive digraphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4.2
arxiv.statement: Let $c(n)$ denote the minimum circumference of a connected vertex transitive graph on $n$ vertices. Let $d(n)$ denote the minimum circumference of a connected vertex transitive digraph on $n$ vertices. Then $d(n)=\Theta(c(n))$.
arxiv.paper_title: Long cycles in vertex transitive digraphs

arxiv.title: Question 4.3
arxiv.statement: Does vertex transitivity guarantee that every two longest directed cycles in a connected vertex transitive digraph share a common vertex?
arxiv.paper_title: Long cycles in vertex transitive digraphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4.2
arxiv.statement: There is a function $f$ such that for every integer $k$, there is a polynomial-time algorithm that, given a tournament $T$, correctly concludes that $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(T)\geq k$, or finds an order $\prec$ of $V(T)$ such that $\omega(T^{\prec})\leq f(k)$
arxiv.paper_title: Finding forest-orderings of tournaments is NP-complete

arxiv.title: Problem 5.1
arxiv.statement: For some integers $q\geqslant 4$ and $N$, does there exist a non-transitive $q$-edge-colored $N$-vertex tournament whose longest color-avoiding path has strictly fewer than $f_{q,q-1}(N)$ vertices?
arxiv.paper_title: Color-avoiding directed paths in tournaments

arxiv.title: Open Question on Circular Drawing Characterisation
arxiv.statement: Is there a theorem that says ``every appropriately connected digraph $G$ contains no thing of type $X$ if and only if $G$ admits a circular drawing''?
arxiv.paper_title: When all directed cycles have the same weight

arxiv.title: Conjecture 6.1
arxiv.statement: All orientations of odd cycles are avoidable.
arxiv.paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs

arxiv.title: Question 6.2
arxiv.statement: Are all digraphs which allow a height function not avoidable?
arxiv.paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs

arxiv.title: Question 6.3
arxiv.statement: Which orientations of $C_{4}$ are avoidable?
arxiv.paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs

arxiv.title: Eulerian-avoidability characterization
arxiv.statement: Which digraphs are Eulerian-avoidable?
arxiv.paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs

arxiv.title: Question 6.4
arxiv.statement: Is every orientation of $C_{4}$ Eulerian-avoidable?
arxiv.paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs

arxiv.title: Problem 23
arxiv.statement: For which finite tournaments $H$, does there exist a countable tournament $G$ that does not contain $H$ as subtournament, yet any tournament obtained from $G$ by changing the direction of a single arc does contain $H$?
arxiv.paper_title: Infinite induced-saturated graphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 24
arxiv.statement: Let $T$ be a finite tournament. If $T$ is not transitive, then there exists a countably infinite $T$-free tournament $S_{T}$ such that every locally finite perturbation of $S_{T}$ has an induced copy of $T$.
arxiv.paper_title: Infinite induced-saturated graphs

arxiv.title: Question 5.1
arxiv.statement: Is it true that every oriented graph $G$ with $\delta^{0}(G)\geq k/2$ contains every orientation of the $k$-edge path, save for the antidirected orientations?
arxiv.paper_title: Two-block paths in oriented graphs of large semidegree

arxiv.title: Question 1.1
arxiv.statement: For which $\varepsilon>0$ and $k>0$ does there exist a bound $c$ such that each simple graph $G$ either contains $C_{2k+1}$ as a subgraph or $\chi(G)\leq c$ (or both)?
arxiv.paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree

arxiv.title: Question 1.2
arxiv.statement: Which orientations of cycles are contained in every digraph with high minimum out-degree and high chromatic number?
arxiv.paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree

arxiv.title: Question 4.1
arxiv.statement: For which fixed digraphs $H$ does there exist a constant $c_{\varepsilon}\geq 1$ for all $\varepsilon>0$ such that every digraph $D$ without loops or parallel arcs with $\chi(D)\geq c_{\varepsilon}$ and minimum out-degree at least $\varepsilon|V(D)|$ contains $H$ as a subdigraph?
arxiv.paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree

arxiv.title: Question 4.2
arxiv.statement: For a fixed digraph $H$, what is the minimum value $\varepsilon_{H}\geq 0$ such that there exists a constant $c\geq 1$ such that every digraph $D$ without loops or parallel arcs with $\chi(D)\geq c$ and minimum out-degree at least $\varepsilon_{H}|V(D)|$ contains $H$ as a subdigraph?
arxiv.paper_title: Orientations of cycles in digraphs of high chromatic number and high minimum out-degree

arxiv.title: Conjecture 1.2
arxiv.statement: Let $G$ be a strongly 2-connected digraph drawn (possibly with crossings) in an annulus $\Sigma$, such that every directed cycle has winding number one around the annulus in the natural sense. Then $G$ can be drawn in $\Sigma$ without crossings, such that every directed cycle has winding number one.
arxiv.paper_title: When all directed cycles have length three

arxiv.title: Informal Question (NP-characterization of weightable digraphs)
arxiv.statement: Can we give a construction for all weightable digraphs (i.e., an NP-characterization)?
arxiv.paper_title: When all directed cycles have length three

arxiv.title: Problem 6
arxiv.statement: Let $\alpha$ be a positive real number. Does there exist a polynomial $P_{\alpha}$ such that $\operatorname{unvd}(D)\leqslant P_{\alpha}(|V(D)|)$ for every digraph with maximum average degree at most $\alpha$?
arxiv.paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 7
arxiv.statement: If $\cal F$ is linearly unavoidable and has bounded maximum average degree, then ${\cal F}[k]$ is also linearly unavoidable.
arxiv.paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments

arxiv.title: Problem 8
arxiv.statement: What is the infimum of all the constants $C$ such that for every large enough integer $n$, the $k$-blow-up of every oriented tree of order $n$ is $(C^{k}\cdot kn)$-unavoidable?
arxiv.paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 9
arxiv.statement: There exists a constant $C$ such that $\operatorname{unvd}(D)\leqslant C\cdot\operatorname{unvd}(D-v)$ for every acyclic digraph $D$ and for every vertex $v$ of $D$.
arxiv.paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 10
arxiv.statement: There is an absolute constant $C$ such that for every integer $k$, every $k$-extension of an oriented tree of order $n$ is $C^{k}(2n-2)$-unavoidable.
arxiv.paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 11
arxiv.statement: If $\cal F$ is linearly unavoidable, then the family of $k$-extensions of digraphs in $\cal F$ is also linearly unavoidable.
arxiv.paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments

arxiv.title: Problem 12
arxiv.statement: What is the minimum function $f$ such that every $k$-extension of every forest of order $n$ is $(2^{f(k)}\cdot n)$-unavoidable?
arxiv.paper_title: Blow-ups and extensions of trees in tournaments

arxiv.title: Open question on strong 2-kernels in split digraphs
arxiv.statement: Does every split digraph $G$ with no sources admit a strong 2-kernel of size at most $|G|/2$?
arxiv.paper_title: Distant digraph domination

arxiv.title: Question 6.1
arxiv.statement: Does every graph $G$ have a spanning subgraph with an Alon-Tarsi orientation in which each vertex $v$ has out-degree at least $\frac{1}{2}(\deg_{G}(v)-1)$?
arxiv.paper_title: List-avoiding orientations

arxiv.title: Problem 1.1
arxiv.statement: What is the minimum integer $f\mathopen{}\mathclose{{}\left(k}\right)$ such that every $f\mathopen{}\mathclose{{}\left(k}\right)$-chromatic oriented graph has an acyclic $k$-chromatic subgraph?
arxiv.paper_title: Acyclic subgraphs of tournaments with high chromatic number

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4.1
arxiv.statement: Every $n$-vertex tournament has an acyclic subgraph with chromatic number at least $n^{3/4-o\mathopen{}\mathclose{{}\left(1}\right)}$. That is, $g\mathopen{}\mathclose{{}\left(k}\right)\leq k^{4/3+o\mathopen{}\mathclose{{}\left(1}\right)}$.
arxiv.paper_title: Acyclic subgraphs of tournaments with high chromatic number

arxiv.title: Conjecture 3
arxiv.statement: $\vec{a}(n)=\Theta(\sqrt{n\log n})$.
arxiv.paper_title: Minimum acyclic number and maximum dichromatic number of oriented triangle-free graphs of a given order

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4
arxiv.statement: $\vec{t}(n)=\Theta\sqrt{\frac{n}{\log n}}$.
arxiv.paper_title: Minimum acyclic number and maximum dichromatic number of oriented triangle-free graphs of a given order

arxiv.title: Conjecture 1.12
arxiv.statement: For every undirected tree, there exists some orientation that has the tournament anti-Sidorenko property.
arxiv.paper_title: Variations on Sidorenko's conjecture in tournaments

arxiv.title: Problem 1
arxiv.statement: What is the value of $r_{t}$?
arxiv.paper_title: Temporalizing digraphs via linear-size balanced bi-trees

arxiv.title: Problem 2
arxiv.statement: What is the maximum $c_{b}$ for which every strongly connected directed graph on $n$ vertices has a balanced bi-tree of size at least $c_{b}.n$?
arxiv.paper_title: Temporalizing digraphs via linear-size balanced bi-trees

arxiv.title: Problem 3
arxiv.statement: Can we compute a left-maximal dfs-tree in $o(n^{2})$ time in minimally strongly connected digraphs? What about the complexity in the general case?
arxiv.paper_title: Temporalizing digraphs via linear-size balanced bi-trees

arxiv.title: Conjecture 1
arxiv.statement: Every strong digraph $D$ on $n$ vertices has a $O(\log n)$ size forward cover.
arxiv.paper_title: Temporalizing digraphs via linear-size balanced bi-trees

arxiv.title: Question 1.4
arxiv.statement: Is $\Delta(1,2,2)$ a hero in oriented complete multipartite graphs?
arxiv.paper_title: Heroes in oriented complete multipartite graphs

arxiv.title: Question 5.4
arxiv.statement: Let $H$ and $F$ be digraphs such that $\Delta(1,1,H)$ is a hero in $Forb_{ind}(F)$ and $H$ is a hero in $Forb_{ind}(K_{1}+F)$. Then $\Delta(1,1,H)$ is a hero in $Forb_{ind}(K_{1}+F)$.
arxiv.paper_title: Heroes in oriented complete multipartite graphs

arxiv.title: Question 10
arxiv.statement: Does there exist, for each integer $d$, an integer $\chi$ such that for every graph $G$ with $\chi(G)\geqslant\chi$ and every tournament $T$ on the same vertex set, there is a vertex $v$ for which $G[N^{+}_{T}(v)]$ has degeneracy at least $d$?
arxiv.paper_title: Chromatic number is not tournament-local

arxiv.title: Conjecture 11
arxiv.statement: For every graph $G$ with sufficiently large chromatic number, and every tournament $T$ on the same vertex set, there exists a vertex $v$ such that $G[N^{+}_{T}(v)]$ contains a cycle.
arxiv.paper_title: Chromatic number is not tournament-local

arxiv.title: Conjecture 12
arxiv.statement: For every positive integer $k$, there exists a $\chi$ such that if $T$ is the uniformly random tournament on vertex set $[N]$, then with high probability (as $N\to\infty$), for every graph $G$ on $[N]$ with $\chi(G)\geqslant\chi$ there is a vertex $v\in[N]$ for which $G[N^{+}_{T}(v)]\geqslant k$.
arxiv.paper_title: Chromatic number is not tournament-local

arxiv.title: Conjecture 13
arxiv.statement: There exists $f(N)$ satisfying $f(N)=o(\log N)$ such that for every $N$-vertex graph $G$ with $\chi(G)\geqslant f(N)$, and every tournament $T$ on the same vertex set, there is a vertex $v$ for which $\chi(G[N^{+}_{T}(v)])\geqslant 3$.
arxiv.paper_title: Chromatic number is not tournament-local

arxiv.title: Question 3.5
arxiv.statement: Is it true that for every tournament $T$, there exists $\prec\in\mathfrak{S}(T)$ such that $\prec$ is both a $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-ordering and a $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\chi}}$-ordering?
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 3.12
arxiv.statement: Let $k\geq 1$. The class of tournaments with twin-width at most $k$ is $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\chi}}$-bounded.
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 3.13
arxiv.statement: There exists a function $f$, such that for every tournament $T$, there exists an ordering $\prec^{*}$ of $V(T)$ such that:
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 3.16
arxiv.statement: There exists a function $f$ such that, for every tournament $T$, there exists a $BST$-ordering $\prec$ of $T$ such:
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4.3 (Gyárfás-Sumner for Tournaments)
arxiv.statement: A tournament $H$ is $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\chi}}$-binding if and only if $H$ has a backedge graph which is a forest.
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4.11
arxiv.statement: Let $(M,\prec)$ be an ordered graph with maximum degree $1$. Then the class of $(M,\prec)$-free ordered graphs is $\chi$-bounded.
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 5.3 (Large dom implies an ω̄-cluster)
arxiv.statement: There exist two functions $f$ and $\ell$ such that, for every integer $k$, every tournament $T$ with $\operatorname{dom}(T)\geq f(k)$ contains a subtournament $X$ with $|X|\leq\ell(k)$ and $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(X)\geq k$.
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 5.6
arxiv.statement: There exists a function $g$ such that, for every integer $t$, if $T$ is a tournament such that for every $v\in V(T)$, $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(N^{+}(v))\leq t$, then $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(T)\leq g(t)$.
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Question 5.9
arxiv.statement: Is there a function $\ell$ such that, for every tournament $T$, if $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(T)\geq k$, then $T$ has a subtournament $A$ such that $|A|\leq\ell(k)$ and $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}(A)\geq k$.
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 5.10
arxiv.statement: For every integer $k\geq 3$, there is an infinite number of $k$-$\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-critical tournaments.
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 6.1
arxiv.statement: If a class of digraphs $\mathcal{C}$ is $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\chi}}$-bounded, then so is its closure under substitution.
arxiv.paper_title: Clique number of tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture (Section 9, characterization of 2-extremal digraphs)
arxiv.statement: [Statement unavailable — Section 9 content absent from PDF extraction]
arxiv.paper_title: Digraph Colouring and Arc-Connectivity

arxiv.title: Open problem on happy triples for $l < k/2$
arxiv.statement: It would be interesting to consider the best upper bound on the number of happy triples in a graph with $k$ edges and maximum degree at most $l$, for the regime when $l < k/2$.
arxiv.paper_title: Improved bounds for the triangle case of Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture

arxiv.title: Conjecture 1.5
arxiv.statement: A tournament has the strong EH-property if and only if it admits a numbering for which the backedge graph is a forest.
arxiv.paper_title: Pure pairs. X. Tournaments and the strong Erdos-Hajnal property

arxiv.title: Informal question: can the D5-freeness hypothesis in Theorem 1.6 be dropped?
arxiv.statement: Perhaps the second sentence in 1.6 (the condition that $H$ is $D_5$-free) can be omitted, but that remains open.
arxiv.paper_title: Pure pairs. X. Tournaments and the strong Erdos-Hajnal property

arxiv.title: Question 1.1
arxiv.statement: Which finite families $F$ of digraphs are $\vec{\chi}$-finite?
arxiv.paper_title: On heroes in digraphs with forbidden induced forests

arxiv.title: Open problem: hero status of $\Delta(1,m,m')$ for degree-4 oriented stars
arxiv.statement: Determine for which integers $m, m' \geq 1$ the set $\{\Delta(1, m, m'), F\}$ is $\vec{\chi}$-finite when $F$ is an oriented star of degree $4$.
arxiv.paper_title: On heroes in digraphs with forbidden induced forests

arxiv.title: Conjecture 1.7
arxiv.statement: For all positive integers $l$ and $k$, $t(l, k) = l^{k-1}$.
arxiv.paper_title: Flashes and rainbows in tournaments

arxiv.title: Conjecture 1.4
arxiv.statement: If $B$ is a bipartite oriented graph with a homomorphism $B \to \vec{K}_2$, then $B$ has the directed Sidorenko property.
arxiv.paper_title: A note on directed analogues of the Sidorenko and forcing conjectures

arxiv.title: Conjecture 1.7
arxiv.statement: If $B$ is an oriented graph with a homomorphism to an edge and whose underlying undirected graph has a cycle, then $B$ has the directed forcing property.
arxiv.paper_title: A note on directed analogues of the Sidorenko and forcing conjectures

arxiv.title: Equivalence of asymmetric and classical Sidorenko properties
arxiv.statement: It is conjectured that the asymmetric Sidorenko property and the classical Sidorenko property are equivalent for bipartite graphs.
arxiv.paper_title: A note on directed analogues of the Sidorenko and forcing conjectures

arxiv.title: Open problem: closing the gap for $d(s)$
arxiv.statement: It remains an interesting open problem to close the gap between the lower and upper bounds: $\Omega(\log s) < \frac{s}{2} - d(s) < O(\sqrt{s\log s})$, where $d(s)$ denotes the largest integer such that every $2n$-vertex digraph of minimum out-degree at least $s$ contains an $n$-vertex subdigraph of minimum out-degree at least $d(s)$.
arxiv.paper_title: Subdigraphs of prescribed size and outdegree

arxiv.title: Question 3.2
arxiv.statement: Is there a function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that for every digraph $D$ with no induced directed cycle of odd length, we have $\vec{\chi}(D) \leq f(\omega(D))$?
arxiv.paper_title: A counterexample to a conjecture about triangle-free induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number

arxiv.title: Open Complexity Problem (2-F-PFC for oriented trees of maximum degree ≥ 3)
arxiv.statement: The complexity of the $2$-$F$-PFC where the underlying undirected tree of $F$ has maximum degree at least $3$ remains partially open. In particular, the complexity for those graphs $F$ where successively deleting all leaves yields a star $K_{1,n}$ is not determined.
arxiv.paper_title: Colorings of oriented planar graphs avoiding a monochromatic subgraph

arxiv.title: Conjecture on r-free digraphs with r > 2n/3
arxiv.statement: All $r$-free digraphs on $n$ vertices with $r > 2n/3$ are at most one edge away from being acyclic.
arxiv.paper_title: Extremal results on feedback arc sets in digraphs

arxiv.title: Suspected directed surplus of a random B-free orientation (Remark 1.6)
arxiv.statement: A random orientation of a $\mathcal{B}$-free undirected graph on $n$ vertices with $m = \mathrm{ex}(n, \mathcal{B})$ edges likely satisfies $\beta(G) = m/2 - \Omega(\sqrt{mn})$.
arxiv.paper_title: Extremal results on feedback arc sets in digraphs

arxiv.title: Open problem on construction size
arxiv.statement: It would be interesting to know if the size of the $(k+1)$-dichromatic example in $\mathcal{C}_3$ can be reduced below the current bound of $n^{2^{\mathrm{poly}(n)}}$, which is larger than the $2^{\mathrm{poly}(|G_k|)}$ bound achieved by Zykov's construction for triangle-free graphs.
arxiv.paper_title: Chordal directed graphs are not $χ$-bounded

arxiv.title: Open problem on parameters governing $\overrightarrow{r}_1(H)$
arxiv.statement: What natural parameters (analogous to degeneracy and vertex count for undirected graphs) determine the growth order of $\overrightarrow{r}_1(H)$ for acyclic digraphs? In particular, is $\overrightarrow{r}_1(H)$ polynomial or super-polynomial in $n$ when $H$ has bounded degree?
arxiv.paper_title: Ramsey numbers of sparse digraphs

arxiv.title: Problem 4.4
arxiv.statement: What is the complexity of $\Sigma$-$k$-\textsc{Dicolourability} for $k \in \{4, 5\}$ and $\Sigma$ different from the sphere?
arxiv.paper_title: On the dichromatic number of surfaces

arxiv.title: Open Problem: exact constant in the exponent
arxiv.statement: It would be interesting to find the exact value of the constant factor in the exponent. Optimizing our proof can yield a lower bound of $n/2^{ck+o(k)}$ with $c \approx 3.9$, but is unlikely to give the correct bound.
arxiv.paper_title: Powers of paths in tournaments

arxiv.title: Problem 1.4
arxiv.statement: Given an oriented matroid $\vec{M}$, decide whether there exists a directed circuit of even size in $\vec{M}$.
arxiv.paper_title: Even Circuits in Oriented Matroids

arxiv.title: Problem 1.2
arxiv.statement: What are the finite sets $\mathcal{F}$ of digraphs for which the class $\mathrm{Forb}_{\mathrm{ind}}(\mathcal{F})$ has bounded dichromatic number?
arxiv.paper_title: Extension of Gyarfas-Sumner conjecture to digraphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4.2
arxiv.statement: Let $H$ be a hero and let $F$ be an oriented forest. The set $\{\overleftrightarrow{K_2}, H, F\}$ is heroic if and only if: either $F$ is the disjoint union of oriented stars, or $H$ is a transitive tournament.
arxiv.paper_title: Extension of Gyarfas-Sumner conjecture to digraphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4.4
arxiv.statement: Given an oriented forest $F$ and for every integer $k$, $\{\overleftrightarrow{K_2}, K_k, F\}$ is heroic.
arxiv.paper_title: Extension of Gyarfas-Sumner conjecture to digraphs

arxiv.title: Research direction: minimal heroic tournament families
arxiv.statement: Characterize all finite and minimal families $\mathcal{H} = \{H_1, H_2, \ldots, H_k\}$ of tournaments such that $\{\overrightarrow{K_2}, \overleftrightarrow{K_2}, H_1, H_2, \ldots, H_k\}$ is a heroic set.
arxiv.paper_title: Extension of Gyarfas-Sumner conjecture to digraphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 1.10
arxiv.statement: There exists a fixed constant $c > 1$ so that the following holds. For every 3-edge-connected oriented graph $G = (V, E)$ of order $n$, every abelian group $\Gamma$ with $|\Gamma| \geq 6$, and every $f : E \to \Gamma$, there exist at least $c^n$ flows $\phi : E \to \Gamma$ with $\phi(e) \neq f(e)$ for every $e \in E$.
arxiv.paper_title: Many flows in the group connectivity setting

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4
arxiv.statement: Let $G$ be a simple $n$-vertex graph and $c$ be a colouring of $E(G)$ with $n$ colours, where each colour class has size at least $r$. Then $(G, c)$ contains a cycle $C$ of length at most $\lceil n/r \rceil$ such that no two incident edges of $C$ are the same colour.
arxiv.paper_title: Short rainbow cycles in graphs and matroids

arxiv.title: Problem 2.15
arxiv.statement: Let $F$ be a fixed (multi-)digraph. Instance: A (multi-)digraph $D$. Decide whether $D$ is circularly $F$-colourable.
arxiv.paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

arxiv.title: Conjecture 2.16
arxiv.statement: Let $F$ be a digraph which contains a directed cycle. Then the circular $F$-colouring problem is NP-complete.
arxiv.paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

arxiv.title: Problem 3.21
arxiv.statement: Let $p\geq 1$ be a fixed real number. Instance: A (multi-)digraph $D$. Decide whether $\vec{\chi}_{f}(D)\leq p$.
arxiv.paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

arxiv.title: Problem 5.40
arxiv.statement: For given $k,b\in\mathbb{N}$ with $k\geq b$, is there a directed graph $\vec{K}(k,b)$ with vertex set $\binom{[k]}{b}$ such that the following holds? The subdigraph of $\vec{K}(k,b)$ induced by any $\{B_{1},\ldots,B_{l}\}\subseteq\binom{[k]}{b}$ is acylic if and only if $\bigcap_{i=1}^{l}{B_{i}}\neq\emptyset$.
arxiv.paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

arxiv.title: Conjecture 1.2
arxiv.statement: For every integer $k \geq 1$, if $G$ is a bipartite digraph, with $n > 0$ vertices in each part, and every vertex has out-degree more than $n/(k+1)$, then $G$ has girth at most $2k$.
arxiv.paper_title: Short directed cycles in bipartite digraphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 1.5
arxiv.statement: For every integer $k \geq 1$, and every pair of reals $\alpha, \beta > 0$ with $k\alpha + \beta > 1$, if $G$ is a non-null bipartite digraph with bipartition $(A, B)$, where every vertex in $A$ has out-degree at least $\beta|B|$, and every vertex in $B$ has out-degree at least $\alpha|A|$, then $G$ has girth at most $2k$.
arxiv.paper_title: Short directed cycles in bipartite digraphs

arxiv.title: Question 1
arxiv.statement: What is the largest minor-closed class $\mathcal{G}_2$ of undirected graphs with dichromatic number at most 2?
arxiv.paper_title: A Note on Graphs of Dichromatic Number 2

arxiv.title: Conjecture 3.5
arxiv.statement: There is an integer $\ell$ such that if $G$ is a directed-triangle-free digraph with $\alpha(D) = \alpha$, then $\gamma(G) \leq \alpha^{\ell}$.
arxiv.paper_title: Domination and fractional domination in digraphs

arxiv.title: Antisymmetric variant of Jaeger's weak 3-flow conjecture
arxiv.statement: There is a constant $k$ such that every directed $k$-edge-connected graph has a $\mathbb{Z}_5$-antisymmetric flow.
arxiv.paper_title: Additive bases and flows in graphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 3
arxiv.statement: There exists a function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ such that $\chi(G) \leq f(k)$ for every $k \geq 1$ and every graph $G$ admitting a spaghetti tree-decomposition $(T, \{B_t\}_{t \in V(T)})$ and a path-decomposition $(P, \{B_p\}_{p \in V(P)})$ such that $|B_t \cap B_p| \leq k$ for every $t \in V(T)$ and $p \in V(P)$.
arxiv.paper_title: Burling graphs, chromatic number, and orthogonal tree-decompositions

arxiv.title: Problem 3
arxiv.statement: For every integer $i \geq 1$, there exists $f(i)$ such that every tournament $T$ with dominating number at least $f(i)$ contains an isomorphic copy of $S_i$.
arxiv.paper_title: Coloring tournaments: from local to global

arxiv.title: Problem 4
arxiv.statement: For every integer $k \geq 1$, there exist integers $K$ and $\ell$ such that every tournament $T$ with dominating number at least $K$ contains a subtournament with $\ell$ vertices and dominating number at least $k$.
arxiv.paper_title: Coloring tournaments: from local to global

arxiv.title: Conjecture 3
arxiv.statement: There exists a least integer $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_k)$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^0(D) \geq \mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_k)$ contains a subdivision of $TT_k$.
arxiv.paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4
arxiv.statement: Every oriented tree is $\delta^+$-maderian.
arxiv.paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

arxiv.title: Conjecture 7
arxiv.statement: If $F_1$ and $F_2$ are $\delta^+$-maderian, then the disjoint union of $F_1$ and $F_2$ is also $\delta^+$-maderian.
arxiv.paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

arxiv.title: Conjecture 11
arxiv.statement: If $T$ is an oriented tree of order $k$, then $\mathrm{mader}_{\chi}(T) \leq 2k-2$.
arxiv.paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

arxiv.title: Problem 12
arxiv.statement: What is $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(\vec{K}_n)$?
arxiv.paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

arxiv.title: Problem 16
arxiv.statement: Are all digraphs $\kappa$-maderian? $\kappa'$-maderian?
arxiv.paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

arxiv.title: Conjecture 2
arxiv.statement: Every digraph has a majority 3-colouring.
arxiv.paper_title: Majority Colourings of Digraphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 9
arxiv.statement: For $k \geq 2$, every digraph has a vertex $(k+1)$-colouring such that for each vertex $v$, at most $\frac{1}{k}\deg^+(v)$ out-neighbours of $v$ receive the same colour as $v$.
arxiv.paper_title: Majority Colourings of Digraphs

arxiv.title: Open Problem 1
arxiv.statement: Is there a constant $\beta < 1$ for which every digraph has a 3-colouring such that for every vertex $v$, at most $\beta \deg^+(v)$ out-neighbours receive the same colour as $v$?
arxiv.paper_title: Majority Colourings of Digraphs

arxiv.title: Open Problem 2
arxiv.statement: Does every tournament have a majority 3-colouring?
arxiv.paper_title: Majority Colourings of Digraphs

arxiv.title: Open Problem 3
arxiv.statement: Does every Eulerian digraph have a majority 3-colouring?
arxiv.paper_title: Majority Colourings of Digraphs

arxiv.title: Open Problem 7
arxiv.statement: A digraph $G$ is majority $c$-choosable if for every function $L : V(G) \to \mathcal{Z}$ with $|L(v)| \geq c$ for each vertex $v \in V(G)$, there is a majority colouring of $G$ with each vertex $v$ coloured from $L(v)$. Is every digraph majority $c$-choosable for some constant $c$?
arxiv.paper_title: Majority Colourings of Digraphs

arxiv.title: Open Problem 8
arxiv.statement: Let $S(G)$ be the set of all stable sets of a digraph $G$ and $S(G,v)$ the stable sets containing $v$. A fractional majority colouring assigns each $T \in S(G)$ a weight $x_T \geq 0$ such that $\sum_{T \in S(G,v)} x_T \geq 1$ for each vertex $v$. What is the minimum $k$ such that every digraph $G$ has a fractional majority colouring with $\sum_{T \in S(G)} x_T \leq k$? Perhaps $k < 3$.
arxiv.paper_title: Majority Colourings of Digraphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 2
arxiv.statement: $\mathrm{Forb}(H)$ is $\chi$-bounded if and only if $H$ is a forest.
arxiv.paper_title: $χ$-bounded families of oriented graphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 4
arxiv.statement: For any oriented star $S$, $\mathrm{Forb}(S)$ is $\chi$-bounded.
arxiv.paper_title: $χ$-bounded families of oriented graphs

arxiv.title: Conjecture 5
arxiv.statement: Let $\mathcal{P}$ be a non-empty subset of $\mathrm{Or}(P_4)$. If $\mathcal{P} \neq \{P^+(3)\}$ and $\mathcal{P} \neq \{P^+(1,1,1)\}$, then $\mathrm{Forb}(\mathcal{P})$ is $\chi$-bounded.
arxiv.paper_title: $χ$-bounded families of oriented graphs
